sources: Gwynne Shotwell interview Jun 2026: ~one Starship launch per month after successful tests; manufacturing ~1 rocket/month aiming toward higher rate
NSF / Starbase tracking: Ship 41 stacking / final assembly; Booster 21 stacked for Flight 14; Ship 42 in production; Booster 22 stacking underway
Public Flight 13 NET / B20 static fire reporting; OLP-1 retrofit for V3 / dual-pad ops
NSF 2026-07-03: Starship Florida / LC-39A pushing for launch this year — deluge tests, chopsticks reeved; B22/S42 could stack at Cape later in 2026 if F13–F14 succeed

goal.statement: One Starship launch per month for the rest of 2026 after Flight 13, subject to test success and FAA clearance
goal._manufacturing_rate_note: Stated Shotwell goal only; cadence floor uses empirical ship-completion gaps

OLP-2.notes: Primary V3 pad (Pad B); hosts F12+ until OLP-1 returns

OLP-1.notes: Pad A retrofit for V3 / dual-pad cadence; earliest dual-pad ops ~Q4 2026

LC-39A.notes: Kennedy LC-39A Starship pad; NSF Jul 2026 — deluge/chopstick progress, ~few months to operational; first Cape flight projection only (does not move Starbase cadence)

vehicles.notes: B20 33-engine static fire complete; Ship 40 tested; NET mid-July
S41 reported fully stacked / final assembly; B21 stacked — first V3 booster catch attempt widely expected
S42 in production (NSF); B22 stacking underway — booster assignment still unconfirmed if catch reuse changes pairings
Pipeline placeholder from manufacturing rate; may shift to OLP-1 once retrofit completes
Pipeline placeholder; dual-pad era if OLP-1 online
